title: Longest Substring Without Repeating Characters
prompt:
Given a string s, return the length of the longest substring (contiguous) without repeating characters.

Notes:
- This is about substrings (contiguous), not subsequences.
- Strings are case-sensitive unless stated otherwise.
- Return 0 for an empty string.
constraintQuestions: Is this a contiguous substring (no gaps), not a subsequence? (Yes.)
Is the comparison case-sensitive? (Assume yes unless told otherwise.)
What should we return for an empty string? (0.)
Can the input contain spaces or symbols, or only letters?
Do we want an optimized O(n) solution (sliding window)?

walkthrough.title: Brute force idea (generate substrings)
walkthrough.body:
Use nested loops to start a substring at every index.
Track seen characters for the current start.
Stop the inner loop when a duplicate appears.
Update longest each time.
Time: O(n^2)
Space: O(n) (seen chars)

codeSnippets.title: JavaScript
C#
Python

walkthrough.title: Sliding window insight (optimal)
walkthrough.body:
Keep a moving window [L..R] that always contains unique characters.
Use a map lastSeen[char] = index.
When you see a duplicate character that is inside the current window, move L to lastSeen[char] + 1.
Always update lastSeen[char] = R and update longest with (R - L + 1).
Time: O(n)
Space: O(n)
